_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-4 Personal MDI Use (Revised 2/14/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-4: Personal MDI Use — MDHHS Approved 2/14/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern the use of a patient's personal metered-dose inhaler (MDI) as a substitute for nebulized albuterol treatment. Personal MDI use is authorized when the inhaler meets all criteria and the patient can effectively use it. 4 puffs from a patient's personal albuterol MDI is considered equivalent to one standard nebulized albuterol treatment.

sections.title: Authorization and Eligibility
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-4: Personal MDI Use — Authorization
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved personal MDI use as an albuterol administration alternative
Personal MDI may be used as an alternative to or in addition to nebulized albuterol treatment
4 puffs of patient's personal albuterol MDI = 1 nebulized albuterol treatment equivalent
Patient must be able to physically coordinate MDI use and breathe in the medication — consider spacer use

sections.title: Required Criteria for Personal MDI Use
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-4: Personal MDI Use — Criteria
points: Inhaler is prescribed to the patient — must be the patient's own prescribed MDI
Medication in the inhaler is albuterol (verify label)
Inhaler is not empty — check canister and patient confirmation
Spacer is preferred when available to improve medication delivery
Patient must be able to coordinate inhalation with actuation — if not, use nebulized albuterol instead

sections.title: Administration Technique
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-4: Personal MDI Use — Technique
points: Instruct patient to exhale fully, then place mouthpiece in mouth (or spacer in mouth)
Actuate MDI at start of slow, deep inhalation
Patient holds breath for 10 seconds, then exhales slowly
Document number of puffs administered, medication name on inhaler, and patient response

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize use of patient's personal albuterol MDI as an administration alternative
options: Authorized — personal albuterol MDI may be used per protocol criteria
Not authorized — personal MDI use not permitted

deterioration_flags: Patient unable to coordinate MDI use — switch to nebulized albuterol immediately
No improvement or worsening after MDI treatment — reassess; consider additional treatments; notify online MC
scope_notes: 4 puffs personal albuterol MDI = 1 nebulized albuterol treatment equivalent
Requirements: prescribed to patient, albuterol confirmed, not expired, not empty
Patient must be able to coordinate inhalation
Document: puffs given, medication name on inhaler, patient response
